name: Dublin Coddle (Irish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the bacon into even-sized pieces, leaving the rind on for added richness.
2. Peel and chop the onions roughly.
3. Peel the potatoes as thinly as possible; if large, cut into two or three pieces, otherwise leave whole.
4. Chop the fresh parsley.
5. Place a layer of onions at the bottom of a heavy pot with a good close-fitting lid.
6. Layer all other ingredients, sprinkling each layer with a grind of fresh-ground pepper.
7. Add no more than 2 cups of water to the pot.
8. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and cover tightly.
9. Simmer for 2 to 5 hours (or cook in a heavy casserole pot at 250°F in a very low oven).
10. If desired, brown the sausages under the broiler before serving.
11. Serve with white soda farl and bottles of stout.
